title: Cloudflare DDoS Mitigation Event Volume by Attack Vector (`cloudflare:json`)
dataSources: Cloudflare Logpush JSON delivered to Splunk HEC/S3 with `sourcetype=cloudflare:json` (HTTP requests, firewall, Workers, DNS, Access as configured)
app: Cloudflare App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4501) + Logpush JSON (`cloudflare:json`)
spl:
index=waf sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"(?i)ddos|mitigat|dos")
| spath MitigationReason
| spath RuleID
| stats count by MitigationReason, EdgeColoCode
| sort -count
description: L3/4 versus L7 attacks need different runbooks. Logpush JSON ties mitigation reasons to colo and rule context.
value: Improves incident documentation for regulators and helps justify advanced DDoS SKUs.
implementation: Configure Logpush jobs per dataset; route all into a unified `cloudflare:json` sourcetype or use transforms to normalize. Use `spath` for PascalCase fields. Create macros per zone name.
grandmaExplanation: We read your Cloudflare edge logs in one place so attacks, misconfigurations, and outages are obvious early.
